description: Primary objective is local score improvement, not automatic Top-3 closure. Use leaderboard only as external context. Slow 5000-segment evaluation is expensive and must be run only after the medium gate is below 43.
title: Challenge: drive local score below 43 before optional slow gate

acceptance_criteria: 1) Default loop is fast gate ./eval/run.sh <controller> 50 8 "42" then medium gate ./eval/run.sh <controller> 100 8 "42,1337,2026"; 2) Run the slow gate ./eval/run.sh <controller> 5000 8 "42,1337,2026" only if the medium-gate anti_fuffa_score is strictly below 43; 3) You may create new controllers from zero and use repo docs/controllers as hints, not ground truth; 4) On timeout or missing deps, retry once, then skip and continue; 5) Leaderboard may be read only to estimate rank, but no submission/proof is required for completion; 6) Task completes only when a local gate strategy produces a validated score below 43 and the result is documented in .ralph-tui/progress.md; 7) Do not mention the literal completion marker in negative contexts.
description: Iterate on existing controllers and create new controllers from scratch when useful. Use repo documentation and current controllers as starting hypotheses only; they may be wrong. Prefer cheap local experiments, and only escalate to the 5000-segment run when the medium gate goes below 43.
title: Iterate controllers with 50->100->5000 gating and new designs

description: Fresh Ralph epic for legitimate local optimization only. This epic supersedes earlier contaminated loops. Use only valid controllers, keep continuous memory in .ralph-tui/progress.md, and avoid simulator/evaluator exploits.
title: Legit local controller loop with hard policy gates

acceptance_criteria: 1) Every promoted controller passes python3 eval/check_controller_policy.py <controller>; 2) Fast gate uses ./eval/run.sh <controller> 50 8 "42"; 3) Medium gate uses ./eval/run.sh <controller> 100 8 "42,1337,2026"; 4) Slow gate uses ./eval/run.sh <controller> 5000 8 "42,1337,2026" only after medium anti_fuffa_score < 43; 5) eval/legit_completion_checklist.md is fully filled before completion; 6) Task completes only when a legitimate controller achieves slow-gate anti_fuffa_score < 43 and .ralph-tui/progress.md records the full evidence; 7) Invalid exploit controllers do not count even if they score better locally; 8) Each incomplete iteration must include at least one exploration branch from a controller family different from the current best legitimate family and at most one exploitation branch on the current best family; 9) Until any legitimate official fast-gate result is below 48, feedback-family retunes (optimized*/robust*/warm/guarded variants) may consume only one branch per iteration; the rest of the iteration must target model-based, learned-policy/distillation, or new hybrid/from-scratch controllers; 10) Each iteration must begin with first-principles hypotheses about the control problem and candidate solution families before deep-reading existing controllers; repo docs/controllers may be used only as secondary reference or implementation detail sources, not as the default starting point.
description: Run an autonomous Ralph loop that searches only legitimate controllers with a mixed explore/exploit strategy. Use fast gate first, medium gate second, and slow gate only after a valid medium score below 43. Keep the bead open across incomplete iterations, use the policy and checklist files, ignore exploit-based results, and do not spend entire iterations only retuning the current feedback baseline. Start from first-principles reasoning about the control problem; treat existing docs and controllers only as secondary reference, not as the default starting point.
title: Search valid controllers until a legit <43 result is proven
